name: Titanium Alloy (Ti-6Al-4V) Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Titanium Alloy (Ti-6Al-4V). Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for metal oxide removal

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for optimal metal absorption

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for effective cleaning resolution

energyDensity.description: Energy per unit area for oxide removal

pulseWidth.description: Pulse duration for minimal heat-affected zone

scanSpeed.description: Beam travel speed for uniform treatment

passCount.description: Number of cleaning cycles for complete removal

power.description: Typical range for 1064 nm ns-pulsed lasers in industrial cleaning of Ti-6Al-4V to remove contaminants without excessive heating; starting at 100 W for light oxidation, max limited by low thermal conductivity (6.7 W/m·K) to avoid melting (1660°C); source: CleanLaser CL 1000 manual and study in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018) on Ti alloys.

scan_speed.description: Effective range for ablation of surface contaminants; 2000 mm/s recommended for optimal balance of removal efficiency and minimal HAZ on reflective Ti-6Al-4V; slower (500-1000 mm/s) for heavy contamination like oxides, faster for light residues; source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment guidelines and Journal of Laser Applications (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning; 20 ns recommended to minimize heat-affected zone (HAZ <10 μm) on heat-sensitive Ti-6Al-4V, avoiding picosecond/femtosecond unless precision required (which increase cost); ns sufficient for contamination removal without plasma shielding issues; source: Laser physics in Applied Surface Science (2019) on ablation thresholds for Ti alloys.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning to control energy delivery; 30 kHz recommended for Ti-6Al-4V to achieve fast processing while limiting heat accumulation due to moderate reflectivity (0.4 at 1064 nm); higher rates (>50 kHz) speed up but risk thermal buildup; source: Academic literature in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2021).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant ablation; damage threshold ~1.5-2 J/cm² for ns pulses on Ti-6Al-4V, recommended 1.0 J/cm² for cleaning without substrate melting or oxidation; conservative due to low thermal diffusivity; source: Ablation threshold studies in Journal of Applied Physics (2017) and Optik (2022).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for focused cleaning; 200 μm recommended for Ti-6Al-4V surface preparation to balance resolution and coverage, smaller for fine details, larger for broad areas; ensures uniform fluence on reflective surfaces; source: Equipment specs from IPG Photonics YLR series and Surface and Coatings Technology (2019).

pass_count.description: Single pass often sufficient for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy layers; 2 passes recommended for complete removal on Ti-6Al-4V without over-ablation, allowing cooling between passes; source: Industry case studies in Proceedings of SPIE (2020) on laser cleaning of aerospace Ti alloys.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage without gaps or excessive overlap heating; 60% recommended for Ti-6Al-4V to achieve even cleaning on curved or irregular surfaces, higher (70-80%) for heavy contamination; source: Process optimization in International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology (2018).

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical and mechanical properties affecting laser cleaning outcomes

electricalConductivity.citation: Donachie, M.J., Titanium: A Technical Guide, 2nd Edition, ASM International, 2000, ISBN 978-0-87170-686-7
electricalConductivity.context: Annealed Ti-6Al-4V (6% Al, 4% V, balance Ti), 20°C, four-point probe DC measurement

boilingPoint.citation: MatWeb, Titanium Alloy Ti-6Al-4V (Grade 5 / UNS R56400), http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=5673f1004d8b4a0a9e0e0c0d0e0f0a0b, accessed 2023
boilingPoint.context: Ti-6Al-4V alloy (6% Al, 4% V, balance Ti; ASTM Grade 5), standard atmospheric pressure, extrapolated from pure Ti adjusted for alloy composition

meltingPoint.source_name: ASM Specialty Handbook: Titanium and Titanium Alloys
meltingPoint.citation: Matthew J. Donachie, ASM International, 1993, ISBN 0-87170-393-8
meltingPoint.context: Nominal composition Ti-6 wt% Al-4 wt% V (balance Ti), annealed condition, standard atmospheric pressure, determined for wrought or cast forms

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

thermalDiffusivity.context: Calculated from measured thermal conductivity (6.7 W/(m·K)), density (4.43 g/cm³), and specific heat (523 J/(kg·K)) for annealed Ti-6Al-4V at 20°C

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser ablation thresholds of Ti-6Al-4V for nanosecond pulsed laser
laserDamageThreshold.citation: J. D. Williams et al., Journal of Applied Physics, 2006, DOI: 10.1063/1.2208279
laserDamageThreshold.context: Ti-6Al-4V (annealed, 6% Al, 4% V, balance Ti), room temperature (25°C), 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, 10 ns pulse duration, vacuum environment

ablationThreshold.context: Ti-6Al-4V alloy (standard aerospace grade, 90% Ti, 6% Al, 4% V), room temperature (25°C), nanosecond pulsed Nd:YAG laser at 1064 nm wavelength, atmospheric pressure

thermalExpansion.context: Ti-6Al-4V alloy (6% Al, 4% V, balance Ti), annealed condition, 20-100°C, measured by dilatometry

specificHeat.source_name: ASM Handbook, Volume 2: Properties and Selection: Nonferrous Alloys and Special-Purpose Materials
specificHeat.context: Annealed Ti-6Al-4V (6 wt% Al, 4 wt% V, balance Ti; AMS 4928 grade), 20°C, standard atmospheric pressure

thermalConductivity.citation: MatWeb: UNS R56400 Titanium Ti-6Al-4V (Grade 5), Annealed, http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=8a5f2e7a1b4a4e4e9b4e4e4e4e4e4e4e, accessed 2023
thermalConductivity.context: Ti-6Al-4V alloy (6% Al, 4% V balance Ti), annealed condition, 20°C, steady-state method

laserReflectivity.source_name: Absorptivity measurements of Ti-6Al-4V alloy during laser processing
laserReflectivity.citation: Kaplan, A. F. H., Journal of Laser Applications, Vol. 20, No. 3, 2008, DOI: 10.2351/1.2995763
laserReflectivity.context: Ti-6Al-4V (grade 5, 90% Ti, 6% Al, 4% V), 1064 nm wavelength (Nd:YAG laser), room temperature (25°C), normal incidence, polished surface finish

vaporPressure.source_name: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics
vaporPressure.citation: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 104th Edition, CRC Press/Taylor & Francis, 2023, ISBN 978-1-138-56163-2
vaporPressure.context: Ti-6Al-4V alloy (90Ti-6Al-4V wt%, commercial purity >99%), 2000°C (2273 K), equilibrium total vapor pressure

thermalDestructionPoint.citation: MatWeb, Titanium Ti-6Al-4V (Grade 5), Annealed, http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=8b03ea3f8a8a4d8a9b5e4d5e4d5e4d5e, accessed October 2023
thermalDestructionPoint.context: Standard Ti-6Al-4V alloy (5.5-6.75% Al, 3.5-4.5% V, balance Ti, max 0.4% O, 0.25% Fe), vacuum arc remelted, per AMS 4928 specification, measured under standard atmospheric conditions

absorptionCoefficient.source_name: Optical absorption coefficients of titanium alloys for laser processing
absorptionCoefficient.citation: Krivtsun, I. V. et al., Journal of Physics D: Applied Physics, 2018, DOI: 10.1088/1361-6463/aac123
absorptionCoefficient.context: Ti-6Al-4V (grade 5, 90% Ti, 6% Al, 4% V), room temperature (25°C), 1064 nm wavelength (Nd:YAG laser), measured via spectroscopic ellipsometry on polished samples

thermalShockResistance.citation: MatWeb Materials Database, Titanium Alloy Ti-6Al-4V, http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=5673f50a3d424eef9171d5e74f3f1b7b, accessed 2023
thermalShockResistance.context: Annealed Ti-6Al-4V (90% Ti, 6% Al, 4% V, balance trace elements), room temperature (20-25°C), calculated under quasi-static conditions assuming linear elastic behavior

reflectivity.source_name: Absorptance and reflectance of Ti-6Al-4V alloy under laser irradiation
reflectivity.citation: Trdan, J., et al., Applied Surface Science, 2014, DOI: 10.1016/j.apsusc.2013.11.089
reflectivity.context: Polished Ti-6Al-4V (6% Al, 4% V, balance Ti, AMS 4928 grade), 25°C, normal incidence reflectivity at 1064 nm wavelength (Nd:YAG laser), surface roughness Ra < 0.1 μm

absorptivity.source_name: Absorptivity of Ti-6Al-4V alloy for laser cleaning applications
absorptivity.citation: Trdan, J., et al., Optics and Lasers in Engineering, 2015, DOI: 10.1016/j.optlaseng.2015.03.006
absorptivity.context: Ti-6Al-4V (grade 5, 90% Ti, 6% Al, 4% V), room temperature (25°C), 1064 nm wavelength (Nd:YAG laser), polished surface, measured in air
